# `models/` — Antimony / SBML exports of the project's closed-form circuits

*Auto-generated by [`scripts/export_lab1_sbml.py`](../scripts/export_lab1_sbml.py). Round-trip-verified by [`scripts/verify_lab1_sbml.py`](../scripts/verify_lab1_sbml.py). Part of the CURE-audit priority list ([`docs/cure-audit.md`](../docs/cure-audit.md) §2).*

Each circuit ships in four formats per the CURE-Reproducible + Understandable + Credible-annotation pillars (Sauro et al. 2025, ref. 99a):

- **`.ant`** — [Antimony](https://tellurium.readthedocs.io/en/latest/antimony.html) source-of-truth; auto-generated by the exporter from the `Circuit` dataclass.
- **`.sbml`** — SBML L3v2 round-tripped via Tellurium + annotated via libsbml with MIRIAM (`bqmodel:isDescribedBy` → PubMed) + SBO terms (species: 0000252 polypeptide chain; reactions: 0000176 biochemical reaction).
- **`.sedml`** — SED-ML L1V4 simulation experiment descriptor (uniform time course, CVODE algorithm KISAO:0000019, one data generator per species).
- **`.omex`** — COMBINE/OMEX archive bundling SBML + SED-ML + metadata.rdf. Master entry is the SED-ML; this is what VCell, BioSimulations, COPASI, and Tellurium each execute directly.

Plus **`regulome_provenance.json`** — the MIRIAM-style manifest for the project's Pando-on-CHOOSE regulome substrate (different model class, not SBML-shaped). Re-emit with `python scripts/emit_regulome_provenance.py`.

## What's here

| circuit | Antimony | SBML | SED-ML | OMEX | Lab 1 cell | canonical reference |
|---|---|---|---|---|---:|---|
| Negative autoregulation (NAR) | [`.ant`](lab1_negative_autoregulation.ant) | [`.sbml`](lab1_negative_autoregulation.sbml) | [`.sedml`](lab1_negative_autoregulation.sedml) | [`.omex`](lab1_negative_autoregulation.omex) | [cell 5](../notebooks/01_gene_circuit_dynamics.ipynb) | Becskei & Serrano 2000, Nature 405:590 — also Lab 1 §2 (Elowitz & Bois). |
| Toggle switch (Gardner-Cantor-Collins) | [`.ant`](lab1_toggle_switch.ant) | [`.sbml`](lab1_toggle_switch.sbml) | [`.sedml`](lab1_toggle_switch.sedml) | [`.omex`](lab1_toggle_switch.omex) | [cell 7](../notebooks/01_gene_circuit_dynamics.ipynb) | Gardner, Cantor & Collins 2000, Nature 403:339 — also Lab 1 §3. |
| Repressilator | [`.ant`](lab1_repressilator.ant) | [`.sbml`](lab1_repressilator.sbml) | [`.sedml`](lab1_repressilator.sedml) | [`.omex`](lab1_repressilator.omex) | [cell 9](../notebooks/01_gene_circuit_dynamics.ipynb) | Elowitz & Leibler 2000, Nature 403:335 — also Lab 1 §4. |
| Positive autoregulation (bistability) | [`.ant`](lab1_positive_autoregulation.ant) | [`.sbml`](lab1_positive_autoregulation.sbml) | [`.sedml`](lab1_positive_autoregulation.sedml) | [`.omex`](lab1_positive_autoregulation.omex) | [cell 11](../notebooks/01_gene_circuit_dynamics.ipynb) | Becskei, Séraphin & Serrano 2001, EMBO J 20(10):2528-35 — Lab 1 §5 exercise (a). |

## How to consume

**Submit an OMEX archive to BioSimulations (any of their bundled simulators):**

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.biosimulations.org/runs \
     -F 'simulator=tellurium' \
     -F 'file=@models/lab1_repressilator.omex'
```

**Submit to VCell directly via vcell-cli:**

```bash
vcell-cli execute --archive models/lab1_repressilator.omex --out-dir results/
```

**Verify any model round-trips through Tellurium + COPASI + matches Lab 1's JAX simulation:**

```bash
uv pip install tellurium     # ~150 MB; pulls libsbml + libRoadRunner + scipy
python scripts/verify_lab1_sbml.py
```

**Load a model into Tellurium:**

```python
import tellurium as te
with open('models/lab1_repressilator.ant') as f:
    model = te.loada(f.read())
result = model.simulate(0, 40, 401)
model.plot(result)
```

**Convert to SBML L3v2:**

```python
import tellurium as te
sbml = te.antimonyToSBML(open('models/lab1_repressilator.ant').read())
open('models/lab1_repressilator.sbml', 'w').write(sbml)
```

## Why Antimony, not SBML directly

Antimony is human-readable; SBML L3v2 XML is not. The committed artifact is the Antimony source-of-truth; the SBML is one `te.antimonyToSBML` call away. This matches the CURE-Understandable §1 recommendation: "explicit representation not intertwined with implementation" — Antimony is the explicit form, libSBML is the lossless XML form.

## What's *not* here, honestly

The Hypergraph Neural ODE ([Lab 5](../notebooks/05_hypergraph_neural_odes.ipynb)) and the cell-state SBI inverse ([Lab 8](../notebooks/08_anatomical_compiler.ipynb)) are *learned* parameterised models — no closed-form SBML representation exists, and trying to force one would defeat the point. The CURE-aligned answer for those is in [`docs/cure-audit.md`](../docs/cure-audit.md) §"structural non-compliance" — emit the anatomical compiler's *output* in a standard format (PhysiCell grammar, per Johnson et al. 2025 — ref. 77c), not the *model* itself.
